description_long: This church features a tall, pointed steeple with a cross at its peak, rising above a robust square bell tower adorned with vertical window slits. The main structure is constructed from reddish-brown brick, with large arched windows along the nave and transept, some of which appear to have decorative framing. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark slate or tile, extending over the main hall and side aisles. The entrance is marked by a prominent arched doorway at the base of the tower, and the building's overall form is elongated with a cruciform layout. Architectural details such as buttresses, window tracery, and the vertical emphasis of the tower suggest a Neo-Gothic style. The church is designed to be a prominent landmark in an urban environment, with features that invite entry and community gathering.
description: A tall, brick Neo-Gothic church with a pointed steeple, arched windows, and a prominent bell tower. The structure features a dark pitched roof and detailed architectural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance at the base of the tall bell tower and the full height of the steeple with a cross on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the nave, large arched windows, and the connection between the main hall and the bell tower.
description_view_2: Rear oblique view displaying the back of the church, additional arched windows, and the full extension of the roof.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite end, showing the transept, more arched windows, and the rear section of the building.
